definition: A WordPress plugin built by Smash Balloon that adds a multi-channel chat widget to your site, supporting WhatsApp, Facebook Messenger, Telegram, and Instagram. It connects your website visitors to the messaging apps they already use, with added features like AI-powered FAQs, Chat Funnels for lead qualification, agent management, and analytics. It works with both the WhatsApp Business App and the WhatsApp Business API -- it's the front-end layer that sits on your WordPress site regardless of which backend you use.
target_audience: WordPress site owners of all sizes who want to add a WhatsApp (or multi-channel) chat presence to their website. No technical skills needed. Particularly useful for small businesses using the WhatsApp Business App who want to professionalize their on-site chat experience, and for API users who want to reduce support volume with on-site FAQs and lead funnels before conversations hit their inbox.
key_features: Multi-platform chat widget: WhatsApp, Facebook Messenger, Telegram, and Instagram in one plugin
AI-powered Smart FAQs with intelligent search -- answers common questions 24/7 without requiring a human agent
Chat Funnels that guide visitors through pre-sales questions and toward purchase decisions
Agent management with individual availability schedules and automatic routing based on who's online
Up to 5 agents on Basic, 10 on Plus, unlimited on Elite
Fully customizable themes, brand colors, header text, and chat assistant icons
Analytics dashboard tracking chat interactions, conversion rates, peak hours, FAQ performance, and agent metrics
Visibility controls for showing/hiding the widget on specific pages or conditions
Smart Search Tokens for AI FAQ functionality (10K on Basic, 25K on Plus, 50K on Elite)
Three-step setup: enter your WhatsApp number, pick a theme, choose where it displays
Works on any WordPress site regardless of whether you use the Business App or API
limitations: WPChat is the on-site widget layer -- it does not replace the WhatsApp Business App or API for actual message delivery
Chat Funnels and advanced analytics require the Plus plan or higher
Smart Search Tokens are finite per plan (10K-50K/year) -- high-traffic sites may need the Elite plan
The plugin routes visitors to WhatsApp (or Messenger/Telegram) for the actual conversation -- there's no built-in live chat hosted on your site
No direct integration with WhatsApp Business API features like templates, broadcasts, or CAPI tracking -- it works alongside those tools, not as a replacement
Branding removal requires the Plus plan or higher
pricing: All plans are annual subscriptions with a 14-day money-back guarantee. Basic: $49/year (1 site, up to 5 agents, 10K Smart Search Tokens, 30 FAQs, 3 themes, basic analytics). Plus: $99/year (5 sites, up to 10 agents, 25K Smart Search Tokens, 100 FAQs, unlimited Chat Funnels, advanced analytics, branding removal, priority support). Elite: $199/year (25 sites, unlimited agents, 50K Smart Search Tokens, unlimited FAQs, priority support). No per-message fees from WPChat itself -- it's a flat annual cost. Compared to BSP subscriptions that run $50-500+/month, WPChat is a very low-cost addition.
scalability_ceiling: WPChat scales well as a front-end widget. The Elite plan supports 25 sites with unlimited agents and FAQs, which covers agencies and multi-site businesses. The limiting factor is Smart Search Tokens (50K max on Elite) for AI FAQ functionality -- very high-traffic sites with heavy FAQ usage might need to monitor token consumption. For the actual messaging capacity, that's determined by your WhatsApp setup (App or API), not WPChat. The plugin doesn't impose message volume limits.
setup_complexity: 1 out of 5. Install the plugin from WordPress, enter your WhatsApp Business number, select a chat theme, choose which pages to display the widget on, and you're done. No API keys, no developer setup, no Meta Business Manager configuration. Adding FAQ content and Chat Funnels takes a bit more time but is entirely done through the WordPress admin with a visual interface.
migration_complexity: Not directly applicable -- WPChat is a WordPress plugin, not a messaging platform you'd migrate to or from. If you switch from the Business App to the API, WPChat continues working exactly the same way since it just routes visitors to your WhatsApp number regardless of what's behind it. No reconfiguration needed when you upgrade your WhatsApp setup.
data_compliance: WPChat itself is a front-end widget and doesn't process or store WhatsApp messages. The actual message data flows through WhatsApp (App or API), so GDPR compliance depends on your WhatsApp setup, not WPChat. The plugin does collect analytics data (chat interactions, conversion metrics), which should be disclosed in your site's privacy policy. The AI FAQ feature processes visitor queries on-site, and Smash Balloon's privacy policy covers how that data is handled. For EU businesses, the key compliance question is on the WhatsApp side (App vs. API), not the WPChat side.
verification_path: Not applicable. WPChat is a WordPress plugin and does not involve WhatsApp verification. Your verification status (Meta Verified, OBA, or none) is determined by your WhatsApp Business App or API setup. WPChat works the same regardless of your verification status.
message_template_rules: Not applicable. WPChat does not send WhatsApp template messages. It routes website visitors to a WhatsApp conversation, where template rules apply based on whether you're using the App or API. WPChat's Chat Funnels and FAQs operate on your WordPress site, not within WhatsApp's messaging system.
messaging_limits: Not applicable from WPChat's side. The plugin has no message volume limits -- it can route unlimited visitors to WhatsApp. Any messaging limits are imposed by WhatsApp itself (256 broadcast cap on the App, tiered limits on the API). WPChat's value here is actually reducing the pressure on your WhatsApp messaging limits by answering questions via on-site FAQs before they become WhatsApp conversations.
ai_automation_policy: WPChat's AI-powered FAQs operate on your WordPress site, not within WhatsApp's messaging platform, so Meta's January 2026 ban on general-purpose AI chatbots does not apply to WPChat's functionality. The AI FAQ feature answers visitor questions on-site before they enter a WhatsApp conversation. This is a distinct advantage -- you get AI-powered customer support without being subject to WhatsApp's increasingly restrictive automation policies.
quality_risk: Not applicable. WPChat operates on your WordPress site and has no interaction with WhatsApp's quality rating system. Your WhatsApp quality rating is determined by how recipients respond to your messages within WhatsApp, not by anything WPChat does. If anything, WPChat helps reduce quality risk by filtering and qualifying visitors before they message you, leading to higher-quality conversations and fewer blocks/reports.
ads_integration: WPChat can serve as the landing experience for CTWA (Click-to-WhatsApp Ads) campaigns. When someone clicks your ad and lands on your WordPress site instead of going directly to WhatsApp, WPChat's widget provides an immediate way to start a WhatsApp conversation, with the added benefit of Chat Funnels that qualify the lead first. This hybrid approach (ad to landing page to WPChat to WhatsApp) can improve conversion quality compared to sending ad traffic straight to WhatsApp. WPChat itself doesn't integrate with CAPI or Meta Ads Manager -- that's handled on the API/BSP side.
ecommerce_features: For WooCommerce store owners, WPChat adds a chat widget that lets shoppers ask product questions, get pre-sales support, and start WhatsApp conversations directly from product pages. The Chat Funnels feature can guide visitors through product selection or sizing questions before routing them to WhatsApp. The plugin doesn't directly integrate with WooCommerce's order system (that's the API/BSP's job for order notifications and cart recovery), but it handles the discovery and pre-purchase conversation layer. Showing the widget on specific product pages or cart pages using visibility controls lets you target high-intent shoppers.
wpchat_relevance: This is WPChat itself. The plugin fills the gap between your WordPress site and your WhatsApp presence (whether App or API). Without WPChat, your options are a basic click-to-chat link, a free widget with limited customization, or building something custom. WPChat brings a professional, branded chat experience with AI-powered FAQs that reduce support volume, Chat Funnels that convert visitors into qualified leads, multi-agent routing so the right person handles the chat, and analytics to understand what's working. It works as the on-site front door for both Business App users (who need all the help they can get with their limited setup) and API users (who benefit from pre-qualifying visitors before they hit the paid API layer).
best_for: Any WordPress site owner who wants to add WhatsApp chat to their site in under 5 minutes
Small businesses using the WhatsApp Business App who want to professionalize their chat presence
WooCommerce stores that want product-page chat widgets for pre-sales support
Businesses with multiple support agents who need availability scheduling and routing
Sites with high FAQ volume that could benefit from AI-powered automated answers
Agencies managing multiple client sites (Elite plan covers 25 sites)
not_suited_for: Businesses that need a full-featured live chat platform hosted on their own site (WPChat routes to WhatsApp/Messenger/Telegram rather than hosting conversations on-site). Also not a replacement for a WhatsApp Business API solution -- if you need templates, broadcasts, CRM integration, or automated message flows within WhatsApp itself, you still need the API and a BSP.
cta_angle: Whether you're on the free WhatsApp Business App or running the full Cloud API, WPChat.com is the missing piece on your WordPress site -- it turns passive visitors into active WhatsApp conversations, with AI FAQs and Chat Funnels doing the heavy lifting before a human ever needs to step in.
